sourceMovie.title: Despicable Me 2
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Despicable Me 2 (2013) stuck because it commits to its animation premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Chris Renaud's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The animation and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Despicable Me
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Despicable Me 2 clicked for you, Despicable Me (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Lego Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Despicable Me 2 clicked for you, The Lego Movie (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Incredibles 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Despicable Me 2 clicked for you, Incredibles 2 (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Despicable Me 2?
faq.answer: Try Despicable Me, The Lego Movie, The Boss Baby—each shares DNA with Despicable Me 2 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Despicable Me 2?
faq.answer: Despicable Me 2 (2013) is best described as Animation, Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Despicable Me 2 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Despicable Me Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
